sections.heading: (1) A Financial Intelligence Centre is hereby  established as an institution outside
sections.summary: Defines 'property' as any property or service derived from unlawful activities.

requirements.text: In terms of section 1 of the Prevention Act, this term means 'any property or any service, advantage, benefit or reward which was derived, received or retained, directly or indirectly, in the Republic or elsewhere, at any time before or after the commencement of this Act, in connection with or as a result of any unlawful activity commenced by any person, and includes any property representing property so derived'.

sections.source_quote: 2. In terms of section 1 of the Prevention Act, this term means 'any property or any service, advantage, benefit or reward which was derived, received or retained, directly or indirectly, in the Republic or elsewhere, at any time before or after the commencement of this Act, in connection with or as a result of any unlawful activity commenced by any person, and includes any property representing property so derived'.

sections.summary: Defines 'money' as any movable, immovable, corporeal or incorporeal thing.

requirements.text: In terms of section 1 of the Prevention Act, this term means 'money or any other movable, immovable, corporeal or incorporeal thing, and includes any rights, privileges, claims and securities and any interest therein and all proceeds thereof'.

sections.summary: Centre must process, analyse information, inform authorities, monitor compliance, and retain information.
sections.dtia_summary: Centre must process, analyse information disclosed to it, inform authorities, monitor compliance, and retain information.

requirements.text: process, analyse and interpret information disclosed to it, and obtained by it, in terms of this Act
inform, advise and cooperate with investigating authorities, supervisory bodies, the South African Revenue Service and the intelligence services
monitor and give guidance to accountable institutions, supervisory bodies and other persons regarding the performance by them of their duties and their compliance with the provisions of this Act
retain the information referred to in paragraph (a) in the manner and for the period required by this Act

sections.source_quote: 4. To achieve its objectives the Centre must- (a) process, analyse and interpret information disclosed to it, and obtained by it, in terms of this Act; (b) inform, advise and cooperate with investigating authorities, supervisory bodies, the South African Revenue Service and the intelligence services; (c) monitor and give guidance to accountable institutions, supervisory bodies and other persons regarding the performance by them of their duties and their compliance with the provisions of this Act; (d) retain the information referred to in paragraph (a) in the manner and for the period required by this Act.

sections.heading: To achieve its objectives the Centre  must-
sections.summary: Centre must process, analyse information, inform authorities, monitor compliance, and retain information.
sections.dtia_summary: Centre must process, analyse information disclosed to it, inform authorities, monitor compliance, and retain information.

requirements.text: process, analyse and interpret information disclosed to it, and obtained by it, in terms of this Act
inform, advise and cooperate with investigating authorities, supervisory bodies, the South African Revenue Service and the intelligence services
monitor and give guidance to accountable institutions, supervisory bodies and other persons regarding the performance by them of their duties and their compliance with the provisions of this Act
retain the information referred to in paragraph (a) in the manner and for the period required by this Act

sections.source_quote: 4. To achieve its objectives the Centre must- (a) process, analyse and interpret information disclosed to it, and obtained by it, in terms of this Act; (b) inform, advise and cooperate with investigating authorities, supervisory bodies, the South African Revenue Service and the intelligence services; (c) monitor and give guidance to accountable institutions, supervisory bodies and other persons regarding the performance by them of their duties and their compliance with the provisions of this Act; (d) retain the information referred to in paragraph (a) in the manner and for the period required by this Act.

sections.heading: (1) The  Centre may do all that  is necessary  or  expedient to  perform  its functions
sections.summary: Centre may do all necessary actions to perform functions, including staff management and banking.
sections.dtia_summary: Centre may determine staff establishment, appoint employees, obtain services, acquire property, open bank accounts, insure itself, perform legal acts, engage in lawful activities, and do incidental acts.

requirements.text: determine its own staff establishment and the terms and conditions of employment for its staff within a policy framework determined by the Minister
appoint employees and seconded personnel to posts on its staff establishment
obtain the services of any person by agreement, including any state department, functionary or institution, to perform any specific act or function
acquire or dispose of any right in or to property, but rights in respect of immovable property may be acquired or disposed of only with the consent of the Minister
open and operate its own bank accounts, subject to the Public Finance Management Act, 1999
insure itself against any loss, damage, risk or liability
perform legal acts or institute or defend any legal action in its own name
engage in any lawful activity, whether alone or together with any other organisation in the Republic or elsewhere, aimed at promoting its objectives
do anything that is incidental to the exercise of any of its powers

sections.source_quote: 5. (1) The Centre may do all that is necessary or expedient to perform its functions effectively, which includes the power to-- (a) determine its own staff establishment and the terms and conditions of employment for its staff within a policy framework determined by the Minister; (b) appoint employees and seconded personnel to posts on its staff establishment; (c) obtain the services of any person by agreement, including any state department, functionary or institution, to perform any specific act or function; (d) acquire or dispose of any right in or to property, but rights in respect of immovable property may be acquired or disposed of only with the consent of the Minister; (e) open and operate its own bank accounts, subject to the Public Finance Management Act, 1999; (f) insure itself against any loss, damage, risk or liability; (g) perform legal acts or institute or defend any legal action in its own name; (h) engage in any lawful activity, whether alone or together with any other organisation in the Republic or elsewhere, aimed at promoting its objectives; (i) do anything that is incidental to the exercise of any of its powers.

sections.heading: (1) The  Minister may remove  the  Director  from
sections.summary: Minister may remove Director from office on grounds of misconduct, incapacity or incompetence.
sections.dtia_summary: Minister may remove Director from office on grounds of misconduct, incapacity or incompetence.

requirements.text: The Minister may remove the Director from office only on the grounds referred to in section 13 or on the grounds of misconduct, incapacity or incompetence

sections.source_quote: 7. (1) The Minister may remove the Director from office only on the grounds referred to in section 13 or on the grounds of misconduct, incapacity or incompetence.

sections.heading: If the Minister has given notice in the Gazette of any  appointment of a person as the
sections.summary: Gazette notice of appointment serves as proof of appointment.
sections.dtia_summary: Gazette notice of appointment may be presented in court as proof of appointment.

requirements.text: If the Minister has given notice in the Gazette of any appointment of a person as the Director or as acting director, this notice may be presented in a Court as proof of the appointment

sections.source_quote: 9. If the Minister has given notice in the Gazette of any appointment of a person as the Director or as acting director, this notice may be presented in a Court as proof of the appointment.

sections.heading: (1) The Director-
(1) The staff of the  Centre consists (of-
(1) No person  other than the Director  may be  appointed or  seconded  to  perform
(1) No person  may be appointed a:$ the Director  of the Centre unless-
(1) The  funds of the  Centre consist of-
sections.summary: Centre's funds consist of parliamentary appropriations, government grants, and legally acquired money.
sections.dtia_summary: Centre's funds consist of money appropriated annually by Parliament, government grants, and other money legally acquired with Minister's approval.

requirements.text: money appropriated annually by Parliament for the purposes of the Centre
any government grants made to it
any other money legally acquired by it, provided that the Centre may accept donations only with the prior written approval of the Minister

sections.source_quote: 14. (1) The funds of the Centre consist of- (a) money appropriated annually by Parliament for the purposes of the Centre; (b) any government grants made to it; and (c) any other money legally acquired by it, provided that the Centre may accept donations only with the prior written approval of the Minister.

sections.heading: The Auditor-General  must audit and report on the  accounts and financial records
sections.summary: Auditor-General must audit and report on Centre's accounts and financial records.
sections.dtia_summary: Auditor-General must audit and report on Centre's accounts and financial records.

requirements.text: The Auditor-General must audit and report on the accounts and financial records of the Centre

sections.source_quote: 15. The Auditor-General must audit and report on the accounts and financial records of the Centre.

sections.heading: (1) The Director may-
sections.summary: Director may delegate powers to staff or instruct staff to perform functions.
sections.dtia_summary: Director may delegate powers to staff or instruct staff to perform functions, with conditions.

requirements.text: delegate, in writing, any of the powers entrusted to the Centre in terms of this Act to a member of the staff of the Centre
instruct a member of the staff to perform any of the functions assigned to the Centre in terms of this Act

sections.source_quote: 16. (1) The Director may- (a) delegate, in writing, any of the powers entrusted to the Centre in terms of this Act to a member of the staff of the Centre; or (b) instruct a member of the staff to perform any of the functions assigned to the Centre in terms of this Act.

sections.heading: (1) The  Council  must-
sections.summary: Council must advise Minister on policies, best practices, and Centre's performance.
sections.dtia_summary: Council must advise Minister on policies and best practices to identify proceeds of unlawful activities, exercise powers of Minister, and Centre's performance.

requirements.text: on the Minister's request or at its own initiative, advise the Minister on policies and best practices to identify the proceeds of unlawful activities
advise the Minister on the exercise by the Minister of the powers entrusted to the Minister in terms of this Act
advise the Centre concerning the performance by the Centre of its functions
act as a forum in which the Centre, associations representing categories of accountable institutions, organs of state and supervisory bodies can consult one another

sections.source_quote: 18. (1) The Council must- (a) on the Minister's request or at its own initiative, advise the Minister on- (i) policies and best practices to identify the proceeds of unlawful activities; (ii) the exercise by the Minister of the powers entrusted to the Minister in terms of this Act; (b) advise the Centre concerning the performance by the Centre of its functions; and (c) act as a forum in which the Centre, associations representing categories of accountable institutions, organs of state and supervisory bodies can consult one another.

sections.heading: (1) The Council consists of the Director  and each of the  following, namely-
sections.summary: Council consists of Director and specified representatives from government bodies and institutions.
sections.dtia_summary: Council consists of Director and representatives from National Treasury, Police Service, Justice Department, Public Prosecutions, Intelligence Agency, Secret Service, Reserve Bank, Revenue Service, accountable institutions, and supervisory bodies.

requirements.text: The Council consists of the Director and each of the following: the Director-General of the National Treasury; the Commissioner of the South African Police Service; the Director-General of the Department of Justice and Constitutional Development; the National Director of Public Prosecutions; the Director-General of the National Intelligence Agency; the Director-General of the South African Secret Service; the Governor of the South African Reserve Bank; the Commissioner for the South African Revenue Service; persons representing categories of accountable institutions; persons representing supervisory bodies; and any other persons or bodies requested by the Minister

sections.source_quote: 19. (1) The Council consists of the Director and each of the following, namely- the Director-General of the National Treasury; the Commissioner of the South African Police Service; the Director-General of the Department of Justice and Constitutional Development; the National Director of Public Prosecutions; the Director-General of the National Intelligence Agency; the Director-General of the South African Secret Service; the Governor of the South African Reserve Bank; the Commissioner for the South African Revenue Service; persons representing categories of accountable institutions requested by the Minister to nominate representatives; persons representing supervisory bodies requested by the Minister to nominate representatives; and any other persons or bodies requested by the Minister to nominate representatives.

sections.heading: (1) The chairperson of the  Council may call  a  meeting of the Council, but  must
sections.summary: Chairperson may call Council meetings but must call a meeting if Minister requests.

requirements.text: The chairperson of the Council may call a meeting of the Council, but must call a meeting if the Minister so requests.

sections.source_quote: 20. (1) The chairperson of the Council may call a meeting of the Council, but must call a meeting if the Minister so requests.

sections.heading: (1) Whenever an accountable institution establishes a business relationship or
sections.summary: Accountable institutions must keep records of client identity, business relationships, transactions, and related details.
sections.dtia_summary: Mandatory record-keeping of client identity and transaction details for financial institutions.

requirements.text: Whenever an accountable institution establishes a business relationship or concludes a transaction with a client, the accountable institution must keep record of the identity of the client.
If the client is acting on behalf of another person, the accountable institution must keep record of the identity of that person and the client's authority to act on behalf of that person.
If another person is acting on behalf of the client, the accountable institution must keep record of the identity of that person and that person's authority to act on behalf of the client.
The accountable institution must keep record of the manner in which the identity of the persons referred to in paragraphs (a), (b) and (c) was established.
The accountable institution must keep record of the nature of that business relationship or transaction.
In the case of a transaction, the accountable institution must keep record of the amount involved and the parties to that transaction.
The accountable institution must keep record of all accounts involved in transactions concluded by the accountable institution in the course of that business relationship.
The accountable institution must keep record of all accounts involved in that single transaction.
The accountable institution must keep record of the name of the person who obtained the information referred to in paragraphs (a), (b) and (c) on behalf of the accountable institution.
The accountable institution must keep record of any document or copy of a document obtained by the accountable institution in order to verify a person's identity in terms of section 21(1) or (2).

sections.source_quote: 22. (1) Whenever an accountable institution establishes a business relationship or concludes a transaction with a client, whether the transaction is a single transaction or concluded in the course of a business relationship which that accountable institution has with the client, the accountable institution must keep record of- the identity of the client; if the client is acting on behalf of another person- (i) the identity of the person on whose behalf the client is acting; and (ii) the client's authority to act on behalf of that other person; if another person is acting on behalf of the client- (i) the identity of that other person; and (ii) that other person's authority to act on behalf of the client; the manner in which the identity of the persons referred to in paragraphs (a), (b) and (c) was established; the nature of that business relationship or transaction; in the case of a transaction- (i) the amount involved; and (ii) the parties to that transaction; all accounts that are involved in- (i) transactions concluded by that accountable institution in the course of that business relationship; and (ii) that single transaction; the name of the person who obtained the information referred to in paragraphs (a), (b) and (c) on behalf of the accountable institution; and any document or copy of a document obtained by the accountable institution in order to verify a person's identity in terms of section 21(1) or (2).

sections.heading: An  accountable institution must  keep the records referred to in section 22 which
sections.summary: Records must be kept for at least five years from termination of business relationship or transaction date.
sections.dtia_summary: Records retention period of five years for financial transactions.

requirements.text: An accountable institution must keep the records referred to in section 22 which relate to the establishment of a business relationship for at least five years from the date on which the business relationship is terminated.

An accountable institution must keep the records referred to in section 22 which relate to a transaction which is concluded for at least five years from the date on which that transaction is concluded.

sections.source_quote: 23. An accountable institution must keep the records referred to in section 22 which relate to- (a) the establishment of a business relationship, for at least five years from the date on which the business relationship is terminated; (b) a transaction which is concluded, for at least five years from the date on which that transaction is concluded.

sections.heading: (1) The  duties  imposed by section  22 on  an accountable  institution to keep record
sections.summary: Third parties may perform record-keeping duties for accountable institutions with access to records.
sections.dtia_summary: Third parties can handle records if accountable institution has access to them.

requirements.text: The duties imposed by section 22 on an accountable institution to keep record of the matters specified in that section may be performed by a third party on behalf of the accountable institution as long as the accountable institution has free and easy access to the records.

If a third party referred to in subsection (1) fails to properly comply with the requirements of section 22 on behalf of the accountable institution concerned, the accountable institution is liable for that failure.

If an accountable institution appoints a third party to perform the duties imposed on it by section 22, the accountable institution must forthwith provide the Centre with the prescribed particulars regarding the third party.

sections.source_quote: 24. (1) The duties imposed by section 22 on an accountable institution to keep record of the matters specified in that section may be performed by a third party on behalf of the accountable institution as long as the accountable institution has free and easy access to the records. (2) If a third party referred to in subsection (1) fails to properly comply with the requirements of section 22 on behalf of the accountable institution concerned, the accountable institution is liable for that failure. (3) If an accountable institution appoints a third party to perform the duties imposed on it by section 22, the accountable institution must forthwith provide the Centre with the prescribed particulars regarding the third party.

sections.heading: A record kept in terms of section  22 (or section  24, or a certified extract of any such
sections.summary: Records produced in court are admissible as evidence of facts contained in them.
sections.dtia_summary: Records from section 22 or 24 are admissible in court as evidence.

requirements.text: A record kept in terms of section 22 (or section 24, or a certified extract of any such record, or a certified printout of any extract of an electronic record) is on its mere production in a matter before a court admissible as evidence of any fact contained in it.

sections.source_quote: 25. A record kept in terms of section 22 (or section 24, or a certified extract of any such record, or a certified printout of any extract of an electronic record) is on its mere production in a matter before a court admissible as evidence of any fact contained in it.

sections.heading: (1) An authorised representative of the  Centre has access during ordinary working
sections.summary: Centre's authorized representatives may access records with warrants for money laundering investigations.
sections.dtia_summary: Centre can access records with warrants for investigations.

requirements.text: An authorised representative of the Centre has access during ordinary working hours to any records kept by or on behalf of an accountable institution in terms of section 22 or section 24, and may examine, make extracts from or copies of, any such records.

The authorised representative of the Centre may exercise the powers mentioned in subsection (1) only by virtue of a warrant issued in chambers by a magistrate or regional magistrate or judge of an area of jurisdiction within which the records or any of them are kept, or within which the accountable institution conducts business.

A warrant may only be issued if it appears to the judge, magistrate or regional magistrate from information on oath or affirmation that there are reasonable grounds to believe that the records referred to in subsection (1) may assist the Centre to identify the proceeds of unlawful activities or to combat money laundering activities.

An accountable institution must without delay give to an authorised representative of the Centre all reasonable assistance necessary to enable that representative to exercise the powers mentioned in subsection (1).

sections.source_quote: 26. (1) An authorised representative of the Centre has access during ordinary working hours to any records kept by or on behalf of an accountable institution in terms of section 22 or section 24, and may examine, make extracts from or copies of, any such records. (2) The authorised representative of the Centre may, except in the case of records which the public is entitled to have access to, exercise the powers mentioned in subsection (1) only by virtue of a warrant issued in chambers by a magistrate or regional magistrate or judge of an area of jurisdiction within which the records or any of them are kept, or within which the accountable institution conducts business. (3) A warrant may only be issued if it appears to the judge, magistrate or regional magistrate from information on oath or affirmation that there are reasonable grounds to believe that the records referred to in subsection (1) may assist the Centre to identify the proceeds of unlawful activities or to combat money laundering activities. (5) An accountable institution must without delay give to an authorised representative of the Centre all reasonable assistance necessary to enable that representative to exercise the powers mentioned in subsection (1).

sections.heading: An  accountable institution and a reporting institution must,  within the prescribed
sections.summary: Accountable institutions must report cash transactions exceeding prescribed amounts to Centre.
sections.dtia_summary: Mandatory reporting of cash transactions above thresholds to Centre.

requirements.text: An accountable institution and a reporting institution must, within the prescribed period, report to the Centre the prescribed particulars concerning a transaction concluded with a client if in terms of the transaction an amount of cash in excess of the prescribed amount is paid by the accountable institution or reporting institution to the client, or to a person acting on behalf of the client, or to a person on whose behalf the client is acting.

An accountable institution and a reporting institution must, within the prescribed period, report to the Centre the prescribed particulars concerning a transaction concluded with a client if in terms of the transaction an amount of cash in excess of the prescribed amount is received by the accountable institution or reporting institution from the client, or from a person acting on behalf of the client, or from a person on whose behalf the client is acting.

sections.source_quote: 28. An accountable institution and a reporting institution must, within the prescribed period, report to the Centre the prescribed particulars concerning a transaction concluded with a client if in terms of the transaction an amount of cash in excess of the prescribed amount- (a) is paid by the accountable institution or reporting institution to the client, or to a person acting on behalf of the client, or to a person on whose behalf the client is acting; or (b) is received by the accountable institution or reporting institution from the client, or from a person acting on behalf of the client, or from a person on whose behalf the client is acting.

sections.heading: (1) A person who carries on a businl:ss or is in charge of or  manages a business  or
sections.summary: Persons must report suspicious transactions or activities to Centre if they suspect money laundering.
sections.dtia_summary: Mandatory reporting of suspicious transactions to Centre.

requirements.text: A person who carries on a business or is in charge of or manages a business or who is employed by a business and who knows or suspects that- (i) facilitated or is likely to facilitate the transfer of the proceeds of unlawful activities; (ii) has no apparent business or lawful purpose; (iii) is conducted for the purpose of avoiding giving rise to a reporting duty under this Act; or (iv) may be relevant to the investigation of an evasion or attempted evasion of a duty to pay any tax, duty or levy imposed by legislation administered by the Commissioner for the South African Revenue Service, must, within the prescribed period after the knowledge was acquired or the suspicion arose, report to the Centre the grounds for the knowledge or suspicion and the prescribed particulars concerning the transaction or series of transactions.

sections.source_quote: 29. (1) A person who carries on a business or is in charge of or manages a business or who is employed by a business and who knows or suspects that- (i) facilitated or is likely to facilitate the transfer of the proceeds of unlawful activities; (ii) has no apparent business or lawful purpose; (iii) is conducted for the purpose of avoiding giving rise to a reporting duty under this Act; or (iv) may be relevant to the investigation of an evasion or attempted evasion of a duty to pay any tax, duty or levy imposed by legislation administered by the Commissioner for the South African Revenue Service, must, within the prescribed period after the knowledge was acquired or the suspicion arose, report to the Centre the grounds for the knowledge or suspicion and the prescribed particulars concerning the transaction or series of transactions.

sections.heading: (1)  A person intending to convey an amount of cash in  excess of the  prescribed
sections.summary: Persons must report cash conveyances exceeding prescribed amounts to Centre.
sections.dtia_summary: Mandatory reporting of cash movements across borders to Centre.

requirements.text: A person intending to convey an amount of cash in excess of the prescribed amount to or from the Republic must, before that person conveys the cash into or out of the Republic, report the prescribed particulars concerning that conveyance to a person authorised by the Minister.
A person authorised in terms of subsection (1) must without delay send a copy of the report to the Centre.

sections.source_quote: 30. (1) A person intending to convey an amount of cash in excess of the prescribed amount to or from the Republic must, before that person conveys the cash into or out of the Republic, report the prescribed particulars concerning that conveyance to a person authorised by the Minister. (2) A person authorised in terms of subsection (1) must without delay send a copy of the report to the Centre.

sections.heading: (1) A report  in terns of section 28,29 or 31 to the  Centre and a  report in terms of  15
sections.summary: Reports must be made in prescribed manner and additional information may be requested.
sections.dtia_summary: Reports must be submitted in prescribed format with additional information possible.

requirements.text: A report in terms of section 28, 29 or 31 to the Centre and a report in terms of section 30(1) to a person authorised by the Minister must be made in the prescribed manner.

The Centre, or an investigating authority acting with the permission of the Centre or under the authority of an authorised officer, may request an accountable institution, a reporting institution or any other person that has made a report in terms of section 28, 29 or 31 to furnish the Centre or that investigating authority with such additional information concerning the report and the grounds for the report as the Centre or the investigating authority may reasonably require for the performance by it of its functions.

When an institution or a person referred to in subsection (2) receives a request under that subsection, that institution or person must furnish the Centre without delay with such additional information concerning the report and the grounds for the report as that institution or person may have available.

sections.source_quote: 32. (1) A report in terms of section 28, 29 or 31 to the Centre and a report in terms of section 30(1) to a person authorised by the Minister must be made in the prescribed manner. (2) The Centre, or an investigating authority acting with the permission of the Centre or under the authority of an authorised officer, may request an accountable institution, a reporting institution or any other person that has made a report in terms of section 28, 29 or 31 to furnish the Centre or that investigating authority with such additional information concerning the report and the grounds for the report as the Centre or the investigating authority may reasonably require for the performance by it of its functions. (3) When an institution or a person referred to in subsection (2) receives a request under that subsection, that institution or person must furnish the Centre without delay with such additional information concerning the report and the grounds for the report as that institution or person may have available.

sections.heading: An accountable institution, reporting institution or person required to make a
sections.summary: Transactions may continue after reporting unless Centre directs otherwise.
sections.dtia_summary: Transactions can continue after reporting unless Centre intervenes.

requirements.text: An accountable institution, reporting institution or person required to make a report to the Centre in terms of section 28 or 29 may continue with and carry out the transaction in respect of which the report is required to be made unless the Centre directs the accountable institution, reporting institution or person in terms of section 34 not to proceed with the transaction.

sections.source_quote: 33. An accountable institution, reporting institution or person required to make a report to the Centre in terms of section 28 or 29, may continue with and carry out the transaction in respect of which the report is required to be made unless the Centre directs the accountable institution, reporting institution or person in terms of section 34 not to proceed with the transaction.

sections.heading: (1) If the  Centre,  after  consulting
sections.summary: Centre may direct non-proceeding with transactions suspected of money laundering.
sections.dtia_summary: Centre can halt transactions suspected of money laundering for up to five days.

requirements.text: If the Centre, after consulting an accountable institution, a reporting institution or a person required to make a report in terms of section 28 or 29, has reasonable grounds to suspect that a transaction or a proposed transaction may involve the proceeds of unlawful activities or may constitute money laundering or may constitute a transaction contemplated in section 29(1)(b), it may direct the accountable institution, reporting institution or person in writing not to proceed with the carrying out of that transaction or proposed transaction or any other transaction in respect of the funds affected by that transaction or proposed transaction for a period as may be determined by the Centre, which may not be more than five days, in order to allow the Centre to make the necessary inquiries concerning the transaction.

sections.source_quote: 34. (1) If the Centre, after consulting an accountable institution, a reporting institution or a person required to make a report in terms of section 28 or 29, has reasonable grounds to suspect that a transaction or a proposed transaction may involve the proceeds of unlawful activities or may constitute money laundering or may constitute a transaction contemplated in section 29(1)(b), it may direct the accountable institution, reporting institution or person in writing not to proceed with the carrying out of that transaction or proposed transaction or any other transaction in respect of the funds affected by that transaction or proposed transaction for a period as may be determined by the Centre, which may not be more than five days, in order to allow the Centre- (a) to make the necessary inquiries concerning the transaction; and (b) if the Centre deems it appropriate, to inform and advise an investigating authority or the National Director of Public Prosecutions.

sections.heading: (1) A judge designated by
sections.summary: Judges may order institutions to report transactions suspected of money laundering.
sections.dtia_summary: Judges can order institutions to report transactions for money laundering monitoring.

requirements.text: A judge designated by the Minister of Justice for the purposes of the Interception and Monitoring Prohibition Act, 1992, may, upon written application by the Centre, order an accountable institution to report to the Centre, on such terms and in such confidential manner as may be specified in the order, all transactions concluded by a specified person with the accountable institution or all transactions conducted in respect of a specified account or facility at the accountable institution, if there are reasonable grounds to suspect that- (a) that person has transferred or may transfer the proceeds of unlawful activities to the accountable institution or is using or may use the accountable institution for money laundering purposes or for the purpose of any transaction contemplated in section 29(1)(b); or (b) that account or other facility has received or may receive the proceeds of unlawful activities or is being or may be used for money laundering purposes or for the purpose of any transaction contemplated in section 29(1)(b).

sections.source_quote: 35. (1) A judge designated by the Minister of Justice for the purposes of the Interception and Monitoring Prohibition Act, 1992, may, upon written application by the Centre, order an accountable institution to report to the Centre, on such terms and in such confidential manner as may be specified in the order, all transactions concluded by a specified person with the accountable institution or all transactions conducted in respect of a specified account or facility at the accountable institution, if there are reasonable grounds to suspect that- (a) that person has transferred or may transfer the proceeds of unlawful activities to the accountable institution or is using or may use the accountable institution for money laundering purposes or for the purpose of any transaction contemplated in section 29(1)(b); or (b) that account or other facility has received or may receive the proceeds of unlawful activities or is being or may be used for money laundering purposes or for the purpose of any transaction contemplated in section 29(1)(b).

sections.heading: (1) If a supervisory  body or  the South African Revenue  Service knows  or  suspects
sections.summary: Supervisory bodies or South African Revenue Service must report suspected money laundering activities to Centre with relevant information.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires reporting of suspected money laundering to Centre with supporting records for investigation.

requirements.text: If a supervisory body or South African Revenue Service knows or suspects an accountable institution has received proceeds of unlawful activities, must advise Centre and furnish all information and records reasonably required
If Centre believes supervisory body or South African Revenue Service may have information indicating money laundering, must request confirmation or rebuttal and furnish information if confirmed

sections.source_quote: 36. (1) If a supervisory body or the South African Revenue Service knows or suspects that an accountable institution... has received or is about to receive the proceeds of unlawful activities... must advise the Centre of that fact and furnish the Centre with all information and any records...

sections.heading: (1) Subject to subsection  (2),  no  duty of secrecy or confidentiality or any other
sections.summary: No duty of secrecy affects compliance with reporting obligations to Centre for money laundering investigations.
sections.dtia_summary: Compliance with reporting to Centre is not subject to confidentiality obligations.

requirements.text: No duty of secrecy or confidentiality affects compliance by accountable institutions, supervisory bodies, reporting institutions, South African Revenue Service with reporting provisions of this Part

sections.source_quote: 37. (1) Subject to subsection (2), no duty of secrecy or confidentiality or any other restriction on the disclosure of information... affects compliance by an accountable institution, reporting institution, supervisory body, reporting institution, South African Revenue Service or any other person with a provision of this Part.

sections.heading: (1) No action, whether criminal  or  civil,  lies  against an accountable institution,
sections.summary: No legal action lies against entities complying with reporting obligations to Centre for money laundering investigations.
sections.dtia_summary: Reporting to Centre for money laundering investigations is protected from legal consequences.

requirements.text: No action, whether criminal or civil, lies against an accountable institution, reporting institution, supervisory body, South African Revenue Service or any other person complying in good faith with a provision of this Part

sections.source_quote: 38. (1) No action, whether criminal or civil, lies against an accountable institution, reporting institution, supervisory body, South African Revenue Service or any other person complying in good faith with a provision of this Part...

sections.heading: A certificate issued by an official  of the  Centre that information specified in the
sections.summary: Certificate from Centre is admissible as evidence in court for reported money laundering transactions.
sections.dtia_summary: Centre's certificates for reported transactions are admissible evidence in legal proceedings.

requirements.text: Certificate issued by Centre that information was reported or sent to Centre in terms of sections 28, 29, 30(2) or 31 is admissible as evidence in court

sections.source_quote: 391. A certificate issued by an official of the Centre that information specified in the certificate was reported or sent to the Centre in terms of section 28, 29, 30(2) or 31 is, subject to section 38(3), on its mere production in a matter before a court admissible as evidence...

sections.heading: (1) No person is entitled  to  information held by the  Centre, except-
sections.summary: Centre may disclose information to specific entities for money laundering investigations with safeguards.
sections.dtia_summary: Centre may share information with investigating authorities, intelligence services, accountable institutions, and supervisory bodies for money laundering investigations with confidentiality safeguards.

requirements.text: No person entitled to information held by Centre except investigating authorities, intelligence services, accountable institutions, supervisory bodies, courts or entities with written agreements

sections.source_quote: 40. (1) No person is entitled to information held by the Centre, except- (a) an investigating authority inside the Republic... (b) an entity outside the Republic performing similar functions... (c) an accountable institution... (d) a supervisory body... (e) in terms of an order of a court; or (f) in terms of other national legislation.

sections.heading: No person may  disclose confidential information  held by or obtained from the
sections.summary: Confidential information held by Centre may not be disclosed except for specific purposes.
sections.dtia_summary: Centre's confidential information may only be disclosed for specific purposes like legal proceedings, compliance with legislation, or with Centre's permission.

requirements.text: No person may disclose confidential information held by or obtained from Centre except within scope of powers, for carrying out provisions of Act, with Centre's permission, for legal proceedings, or in terms of court order

sections.source_quote: 41. No person may disclose confidential information held by or obtained from the Centre except- (a) within the scope of that person's powers and duties in terms of any legislation; (b) for the purpose of carrying out the provisions of this Act; (c) with the permission of the Centre; (d) for the purpose of legal proceedings... (e) in terms of an order of court.

sections.heading: (1) An accountable institution must formulate and implement internal rules
sections.summary: Accountable institutions must create internal rules for identifying persons, record-keeping, and transaction reporting.
sections.dtia_summary: Accountable institutions must implement internal rules for identifying persons, record-keeping, and transaction reporting to comply with money laundering regulations.

requirements.text: Accountable institution must formulate and implement internal rules concerning establishment and verification of identity of persons, information to be kept, manner of keeping records, steps to determine reportable transactions

sections.source_quote: 42. (1) An accountable institution must formulate and implement internal rules concerning- (a) the establishment and verification of the identity of persons whom the institution must identify in terms of Part 1 of this Chapter; (b) the information of which record must be kept in terms of Part 2 of this Chapter; (c) the manner in which and place at which such records must be kept; (d) the steps to be taken to determine when a transaction is reportable to ensure the institution complies with its duties under this Act; and (e) such other matters as may be prescribed.

sections.heading: An accountable institution must-
sections.summary: Accountable institutions must provide training and appoint compliance officers.
sections.dtia_summary: Accountable institutions must train employees and appoint compliance officers to ensure adherence to money laundering regulations.

requirements.text: Accountable institution must provide training to employees to enable compliance with provisions of Act and internal rules
Accountable institution must appoint a person responsible for ensuring compliance with provisions of Act and internal rules

sections.source_quote: 43. An accountable institution must- (a) provide training to its employees, to enable them to comply with the provisions of this Act and the internal rules applicable to them; (b) appoint a person with the responsibility to ensure compliance by- (i) the employees of the accountable institution with the provisions of this Act and the internal rules applicable to them; (ii) the accountable institution with its obligations under this Act.

sections.heading: If the  Centre in the performance of  it,s functions  has  reasonable  grounds
sections.summary: Centre may refer suspected non-compliance to supervisory bodies or investigating authorities.
sections.dtia_summary: Centre may refer suspected money laundering non-compliance to supervisory bodies or investigating authorities for remedial action.

requirements.text: If Centre has reasonable grounds to suspect non-compliance, may refer matter to relevant investigating authority or supervisory body

sections.source_quote: 44. If the Centre in the performance of its functions has reasonable grounds to suspect that an accountable institution... has contravened or failed to comply with any provision of this Act... it may, if it considers it appropriate to do so, refer the matter to- (a) a relevant investigating authority; or (b) an appropriate supervisory body or other public body or authority detected by it...

sections.heading: (1) Each supervisory body is responsible for supervising compliance with the
sections.summary: Supervisory bodies must investigate Centre referrals and remedy suspected non-compliance.
sections.dtia_summary: Supervisory bodies must investigate Centre referrals and take steps to remedy suspected money laundering non-compliance.

requirements.text: Each supervisory body is responsible for supervising compliance with provisions of this Chapter by accountable institutions regulated by it

sections.source_quote: 45. (1) Each supervisory body is responsible for supervising compliance with the provisions of this Chapter by each accountable institution regulated or supervised by it.

sections.heading: (1) An accountable institution  that  performs any act to give effect to a  business
sections.summary: Accountable institutions that perform transactions in contravention of identification requirements are guilty of offences.
sections.dtia_summary: Accountable institutions that fail to identify persons in transactions are subject to offences under this Act.

requirements.text: Accountable institution that performs any act to give effect to a business relationship or single transaction in contravention of section 21(1) is guilty of an offence

sections.source_quote: 46. (1) An accountable institution that performs any act to give effect to a business relationship or single transaction in contravention of section  21(1) is guilty of an offence.

sections.heading: An accountable institution  that  fails  tc+-
sections.summary: Accountable institutions that fail to keep records as required are guilty of offences.
sections.dtia_summary: Accountable institutions that fail to maintain records as required by the Act are subject to offences.

requirements.text: Accountable institution that fails to keep record of information in terms of section 22(1); or keep such records in accordance with section 23 or 24(1); or comply with section 24(3) is guilty of an offence

sections.source_quote: 47. An accountable institution that fails to- (a) keep record of information in terms of section 22(1); or (b) keep such records in accordance with section 23 or section 24(1); or (c) comply with the provisions of section 24(3), is guilty of an offence.

sections.heading: Any person who wilfully tampers with a  record  kept in terms of section  22 or
sections.summary: Wilful tampering or destruction of records is an offence.
sections.dtia_summary: Wilful tampering or destruction of records required by the Act is subject to criminal penalties.

requirements.text: Any person who wilfully tampers with a record kept in terms of section 22 or section 24(1), or wilfully destroys such a record, otherwise than in accordance with section 23, is guilty of an offence

sections.source_quote: 48. Any person who wilfully tampers with a record kept in terms of section 22 or section 24(1), or wilfully destroys such a record, otherwise than in accordance with section 23, is guilty of an offence.

sections.heading: An accountable institution that  fails to give  assistance to a  representative of the
sections.summary: Accountable institutions that fail to assist Centre representatives are guilty of offences.
sections.dtia_summary: Accountable institutions that fail to assist Centre representatives with investigations are subject to offences.

requirements.text: Accountable institution that fails to give assistance to a representative of the Centre in accordance with section 26(5) is guilty of an offence

sections.source_quote: 49. An accountable institution that fails to give assistance to a representative of the Centre in accordance with section 26(5), is guilty of an offence.

sections.heading: An accountable institution or reporting  institution that fails, within the  prescribed
sections.summary: Accountable institutions that fail to report cash transactions are guilty of offences.
sections.dtia_summary: Accountable institutions that fail to report cash transactions within prescribed periods are subject to offences.

requirements.text: Accountable institution or reporting institution that fails, within the prescribed period, to report to the Centre the prescribed information in respect of a cash transaction in accordance with section 28 is guilty of an offence

sections.source_quote: 51. An accountable institution or reporting institution that fails, within the prescribed period, to report to the Centre the prescribed information in respect of a cash transaction in accordance with section 28, is guilty of an offence.

sections.heading: (1) Any  person who  fails, within the: prescribed period, to report to the Centre the
sections.summary: Failure to report suspicious transactions is an offence.
sections.dtia_summary: Failure to report suspicious transactions within prescribed periods is subject to criminal penalties.

requirements.text: Any person who fails, within the prescribed period, to report to the Centre the prescribed information in respect of a suspicious or unusual transaction or series of transactions or enquiry in accordance with section 29(1) or (2) is guilty of an offence

sections.source_quote: 52. (1) Any person who fails, within the prescribed period, to report to the Centre the prescribed information in respect of a suspicious or unusual transaction or series of transactions or enquiry in accordance with section 29(1) or (2), is guilty of an offence.

sections.heading: (1) Any  person  referred to  in  section  29(3)  who discloses a  fact or information
sections.summary: Unauthorized disclosure of information to Centre is an offence.
sections.dtia_summary: Unauthorized disclosure of information to Centre is subject to criminal penalties.

requirements.text: Any person who discloses a fact or information contemplated in section 29(3) otherwise than in the circumstances or for the purposes authorised in that section is guilty of an offence

sections.source_quote: 53. (1) Any person referred to in section 29(3) who discloses a fact or information contemplated in that section, otherwise than in the circumstances or for the purposes authorised in that section, is guilty of an offence.

sections.heading: Any  person who wilfully fails  to  report  the conveyance of cash  into  or out of the
sections.summary: Wilful failure to report cash conveyance is an offence.
sections.dtia_summary: Wilful failure to report cash conveyance into or out of Republic is subject to criminal penalties.

requirements.text: Any person who wilfully fails to report the conveyance of cash into or out of the Republic in accordance with section 30(1) is guilty of an offence

sections.source_quote: 54. Any person who wilfully fails to report the conveyance of cash into or out of the Republic in accordance with section 30(1), is guilty of an offence.

sections.heading: A person referred to in rsection 30(2) who fails to send a report regarding the
sections.summary: Failure to send cash conveyance reports is an offence.
sections.dtia_summary: Failure to send cash conveyance reports to Centre is subject to criminal penalties.

requirements.text: A person referred to in section 30(2) who fails to send a report regarding the conveyance of cash to the Centre in accordance with that section is guilty of an offence

sections.source_quote: 55. A person referred to in section 30(2) who fails to send a report regarding the conveyance of cash to the Centre in accordance with that section, is guilty of an offence.

sections.heading: An  accountable institution, reporting, institution or  any other person that fails to
sections.summary: Accountable institutions failing to comply with Centre's requests are guilty of an offence.
sections.dtia_summary: Offence for non-compliance with Centre's requests; requires adherence to Centre's directives.

requirements.text: comply with a request made by Centre or investigating authority acting under authority of an authorised office in terms of section 32(2)

sections.source_quote: An accountable institution, reporting, institution or any other person that fails to comply with a request made by Centre or an investigating authority acting under the authority of an authorised office in terms of section 32(2), is guilty of an offence.

sections.heading: An  accountable institution that fails 'to comply  with a  direction by the  Centre in
sections.summary: Accountable institutions failing to comply with Centre's directions are guilty of an offence.
sections.dtia_summary: Offence for non-compliance with Centre's directions; requires adherence to Centre's directives.

requirements.text: comply with a direction by Centre in terms of section 34(1)

sections.source_quote: An accountable institution that fails to comply with a direction by Centre in terms of section 34(1), is guilty of an offence.

sections.heading: (1) Any  person who-
sections.summary: Offences for disclosing, tampering with, or misusing Centre's confidential information.
sections.dtia_summary: Offence for disclosing, tampering with, or misusing Centre's confidential information; requires adherence to Centre's information handling rules.

requirements.text: disclose confidential information held by or obtained from Centre
wilfully destroy or tamper with information kept by Centre
use information obtained from Centre otherwise than in accordance with section 40

sections.source_quote: Any person who discloses confidential information held by or obtained from Centre; wilfully destroys or tampers with information kept by Centre; or uses information obtained from Centre otherwise than in accordance with section 40, is guilty of an offence.

sections.heading: An  accountable institution that fails to-
sections.summary: Accountable institutions failing to implement internal rules are guilty of an offence.
sections.dtia_summary: Offence for failing to formulate and implement internal rules; requires adherence to internal rules for compliance.

requirements.text: formulate and implement internal rules in accordance with section 42(1)
make internal rules available to employees in accordance with section 42(3)
make a copy of internal rules available to Centre or supervisory body in accordance with section 42(4)

sections.source_quote: An accountable institution that fails to formulate and implement internal rules in accordance with section 42(1) and make internal rules available to employees in accordance with section 42(3); or make a copy of internal rules available to Centre or supervisory body in accordance with section 42(4), is guilty of an offence.

sections.heading: An accountable institution that fails to-
sections.summary: Accountable institutions failing to provide training or appoint compliance officers are guilty of an offence.
sections.dtia_summary: Offence for failing to provide employee training or appoint compliance officers; requires adherence to training and compliance officer requirements.

requirements.text: provide training to employees in accordance with section 43(a)
appoint the person referred to in section 43(b)

sections.source_quote: An accountable institution that fails to provide training to its employees in accordance with section 43(a); or appoint the person referred to in section 43(b), is guilty of an offence.

sections.heading: Any  person who obstructs, hinders  or  threatens  an official or  representative of the
sections.summary: Obstructing Centre officials in performing duties is an offence.
sections.dtia_summary: Offence for obstructing Centre officials; requires non-obstruction of Centre officials' duties.

requirements.text: obstruct, hinder or threaten Centre officials in performance of duties

sections.source_quote: Any person who obstructs, hinders or threatens Centre officials in the performance of their duties or exercise of powers in terms of this Act, is guilty of an offence.

sections.heading: Any  person who  conducts, or causes  to  be  conducted,  two or more transactions
sections.summary: Conducting transactions to avoid reporting duties is an offence.
sections.dtia_summary: Offence for conducting transactions to avoid reporting duties; requires full reporting of transactions.

requirements.text: conduct two or more transactions with purpose to avoid reporting duty under Act

sections.source_quote: Any person who conducts, or causes to be conducted, two or more transactions with the purpose, in whole or in part, of avoiding giving rise to a reporting duty under this Act, is guilty of an offence.

sections.heading: (1) Any person  who,  without authority to do so, wilfully  accesses  or causes any 35
sections.summary: Unauthorised access to Centre's computer systems is an offence.
sections.dtia_summary: Offence for unauthorised access to Centre's computer systems; requires adherence to access controls.

requirements.text: without authority to do so, wilfully accesses or causes any person to access Centre's computer system

sections.source_quote: Any person who, without authority to do so, wilfully accesses or causes any other person to access Centre's computer system, is guilty of an offence.

sections.heading: Any  person who, without  authority ':o do so, wilfully causes  a computer  system
sections.summary: Unauthorised modification of Centre's computer systems is an offence.
sections.dtia_summary: Offence for unauthorised modification of Centre's computer systems; requires adherence to system integrity.

requirements.text: without authority to do so, wilfully causes Centre's computer system to be modified, destroyed, erased or operation impaired

sections.source_quote: Any person who, without authority to do so, wilfully causes Centre's computer system to be modified, destroyed, erased or operation impaired, is guilty of an offence.

sections.heading: For the purposes of sections 65 and 66-
sections.summary: Defines key terms for computer systems and data handling.
sections.dtia_summary: Defines terms like 'access', 'application', 'computer system', and 'data' for compliance purposes.
sections.source_quote: For the purposes of sections 65 and 66: 'access' means rendering application or data in a form that enables a person to take account of it; 'application' means set of instructions for computer system; 'computer system' means electronic data processing device; 'data' means representation of information capable of being processed in a computer system.

sections.heading: (1) A person convicted of an offence mentioned in this Chapter, other than an
sections.summary: Penalties for offences under this Act, including imprisonment and fines.
sections.dtia_summary: Penalties for non-compliance: imprisonment up to 15 years or fine up to R10 million for most offences; specific penalties for certain offences.

requirements.text: liable to imprisonment for a period not exceeding 15 years or fine not exceeding R10,000,000

sections.source_quote: A person convicted of an offence mentioned in this Chapter, other than an offence mentioned in subsection (2), is liable to imprisonment for a period not exceeding 15 years or to a fine not exceeding R10,000,000.

sections.heading: If a person who is an employee, director or trustee
sections.summary: Defences for employees, directors, or trustees of accountable institutions charged with offences.
sections.dtia_summary: Defences for employees/directors/trustees: compliance with internal rules, reporting to responsible person, or reporting to superior.

requirements.text: had complied with applicable obligations in internal rules relating to reporting of information
reported the matter to the person charged with responsibility for compliance
reported the matter to superior if applicable

sections.source_quote: If a person who is an employee, director or trustee of an accountable institution is charged with committing an offence under section 52, that person may raise as a defence the fact that he or she had complied with applicable obligations in internal rules relating to reporting of information; or reported the matter to the person charged with responsibility for compliance; or reported the matter to superior if applicable.

sections.heading: t(1) A  police official or person authorised by the  Minister  to  receive  a  report  under
sections.summary: Search, seizure, and forfeiture of cash related to financial intelligence.
sections.dtia_summary: Procedures for searching, seizing, and forfeiting cash suspected of being involved in money laundering.

requirements.text: search any person, container or other thing in which cash is suspected to be found
seize any cash suspected to be involved in money laundering
return seized cash to person within 90 days unless specific conditions apply

sections.source_quote: A police official or person authorised by the Minister to receive a report under section 30(1), who has reasonable grounds to suspect that an offence under section 54 has been or is about to be committed, may search any person, container or other thing in which cash is suspected to be found.

sections.heading: (1) A  regional court has  penal jurisdiction  to  impose any  penalty  mentioned in
sections.summary: Courts have jurisdiction to impose penalties and forfeiture orders.
sections.dtia_summary: Courts have jurisdiction to impose penalties and forfeiture orders even if amounts exceed their jurisdictional limits.

requirements.text: regional court has penal jurisdiction to impose penalties mentioned in section 68(1)
magistrate's court has penal jurisdiction to impose penalties mentioned in section 68(2)
magistrate's court or regional court has jurisdiction to make forfeiture orders

sections.source_quote: A regional court has penal jurisdiction to impose any penalty mentioned in section 68(1), even though that penalty may exceed the penal jurisdiction of that court.

sections.heading: This Act does not detract from-
sections.summary: Amendments to the list of accountable institutions by the Minister.
sections.dtia_summary: Minister may amend list of accountable institutions by Gazette notice after consulting Council and Centre.

requirements.text: amend list of accountable institutions in Schedule 1 by notice in Gazette

sections.source_quote: The Minister may, by notice in the Gazette, amend the list of accountable institutions in Schedule 1 to add, delete, or make technical changes to the list after consulting Council and Centre.

sections.heading: (1) The Minister may, after consulting the Council and the Centre, and on
sections.summary: Courts have jurisdiction to impose penalties and forfeiture orders.
sections.dtia_summary: Courts have jurisdiction to impose penalties and forfeiture orders even if amounts exceed their jurisdictional limits.

requirements.text: regional court has penal jurisdiction to impose penalties mentioned in section 68(1)
magistrate's court has penal jurisdiction to impose penalties mentioned in section 68(2)
magistrate's court or regional court has jurisdiction to make forfeiture orders

sections.source_quote: A regional court has penal jurisdiction to impose any penalty mentioned in section 68(1), even though that penalty may exceed the penal jurisdiction of that court.

sections.heading: (1) The Minister may, by notice in the Gazette, amend the list of supervisory
(1) The Minister may, by notice in the Gazette, amend the list of reporting
sections.summary: Minister may amend reporting institutions list via Gazette notice with conditions for additions/deletions and parliamentary approval.

requirements.text: Minister may amend list of reporting institutions by adding or deleting persons/categories
Minister must give 30 days' written notice to affected person for single case
Minister must give 60 days' written notice to category of persons for group cases
Amendments must be approved by Parliament before Gazette publication

sections.source_quote: 76. (1) The Minister may, by notice in the Gazette, amend the list of reporting institutions in Schedule 3

sections.heading: (1) The Minister, after consulting  the  Council and the Centre,  may  make,  repeal
sections.summary: Minister may make/repeal/amend regulations after consulting Council and Centre, with specific provisions for different institutions and penalties.

requirements.text: Regulations may differ for different institutions or categories
Regulations may be limited to specific institutions or categories
Regulations may prescribe imprisonment up to 6 months or fine up to R100 000 for contraventions
Regulations must be reviewed by Council within two years of publication
Regulations must be tabled in Parliament before Gazette publication

sections.source_quote: 77. (1) The Minister, after consulting the Council and the Centre, may make, repeal and amend regulations concerning any matter

sections.heading: The  Minister, the  Centre or  an employee or representative of the Centre,  or  any
sections.summary: Persons performing functions under the Act are not liable for actions taken in good faith.

requirements.text: Persons performing functions under Act are not liable for actions taken in good faith

sections.source_quote: 78. The Minister, the Centre or an employee or representative of the Centre, or any other person performing a function or exercising a power in terms of this Act, is not liable for anything done in good faith in terms of or in furthering the objectives of this Act.

sections.heading: The footnotes in this Act have been  inserted only  for  ease of reference to relevant  25
sections.summary: Footnotes in the Act are for reference only and do not have legal force.
sections.source_quote: 80. The footnotes in this Act have been inserted only for ease of reference to relevant provisions of the Prevention Act. They are not part of this Act. They do not have the force of law.

sections.heading: (1) Until the date refened to in section 82(2), the person designated for the
sections.summary: Designated persons continue to hold office until section 82(2) date, with transitional provisions for pending cases.

requirements.text: Designated persons continue to hold office as if Act had not been passed
Pending proceedings under Prevention Act must be dealt with as if Act had not been passed

sections.source_quote: 81. (1) Until the date referred to in section 82(2), the person designated for the purposes of section 7 of the Prevention Act will be deemed to have been duly designated and will continue to hold office as if this Act had not been passed.

sections.heading: (1) This Act is called the Financial Intelligence  Centre  Act, 2001, and  takes effect
sections.summary: Act takes effect on date fixed by President, with specific transitional provisions for sections.

requirements.text: Act takes effect on date fixed by President by proclamation
Section 79 does not take effect before section 29 takes effect
Section 21(2) takes effect one year after section 21(1) takes effect

sections.source_quote: 82. (1) This Act is called the Financial Intelligence Centre Act, 2001, and takes effect on a date fixed by the President by proclamation in the Gazette.

sections.heading: An  attorney  as  defined in the Attorneys Act, 1979 (Act 53 of 1979).
sections.summary: Defines accountable institutions under FICA, including banks, insurance companies, and other financial entities.

requirements.text: Accountable institutions include banks, insurance companies, and other financial entities

sections.source_quote: 19. An attorney as defined in the Attorneys Act, 1979 (Act 53 of 1979). A person who carries on the business of a bank as defined in the Banks Act, 1990 (Act 94 of 1990).

sections.summary: Defines supervisory bodies under FICA, including financial regulators and authorities.

requirements.text: Supervisory bodies include Financial Services Board, South African Reserve Bank, etc.

sections.source_quote: 1. The Financial Services Board established by the Financial Services Board Act, 1990 (Act 97 of 1990).

sections.heading: The substitution for section 7A of the following section:
sections.summary: Amends section 7A of Prevention of Organised Crime Act with additional defences for reporting obligations.

requirements.text: Defence may be raised if person reported knowledge/suspicion under FICA
Defence may be raised if person complied with internal reporting rules
Defence may be raised if person reported to responsible person

sections.source_quote: 2. The substitution for section 7A of the following section: Defence 7A. (1) If a person is charged with committing an offence under section 2(1)(a) or (b), 4, 5 or 6, that person may raise as a defence the fact that he or she had reported a knowledge or suspicion in terms of section 29 of the Financial Intelligence Centre Act, 2001.

sections.heading: The amendment of section 77-
sections.summary: Amends section 77 of FICA by deleting specific regulatory provisions.

requirements.text: Deletion from subsection (1) of paragraph (b) of section 77
Deletion from subsection (1) of paragraph (c) of section 77

sections.source_quote: 4. The amendment of section 77- 3.1 by the deletion from subsection (1) of paragraph (b); and 3.2 by the deletion from subsection (1) of paragraph (c).
